_doc:
Static-fact intent cache. STT output is lowercased and substring-matched against each intent's `trigger_phrases`. Longest-trigger-wins to disambiguate overlapping intents (e.g. 'pet fee' beats 'pet'). On hit: a response variant is picked at random (weighted, avoiding the most-recently-used response within the same call, and filtered to only those allowed for the agent's current persona). The picked text is spoken via the TTS audio cache, skipping the LLM entirely. The (user-STT-text, chosen-response-text) pair is injected into the LLM's chat history so subsequent turns have continuity — e.g. if the cache fires on 'do you allow dogs?' with a $20-fee answer, a later LLM-driven price quote will correctly include the pet fee.

Philosophy: only intents where the answer doesn't change with the date or who is calling. Reservation lookups, availability, and booking flows MUST go to the LLM. Most responses are bare factual answers — booking-funnel follow-ups are deliberately rare because the LLM has been overdoing them, and the sale should be a byproduct of good conversation rather than the goal of every turn.

Response schema: `text` (required), `weight` (optional, default 1.0), `personas` (optional list; if absent, response is persona-agnostic and used regardless of which voice/persona Iris is running as). A response with `personas: ['Iris']` will only be picked when Iris is the active persona; it'll be filtered out when running as Henry, Aoede, or Eric. Used to prevent mined responses containing 'Iris' / 'Henry' / 'Sadie' / 'Aoede' / 'Eric' from leaking into the wrong persona's mouth. Mining pipeline auto-flags any response containing a persona name; ungraded ones don't enter the cache until tagged or rewritten persona-agnostic.

Prewarm: every response.text in this file gets pre-rendered into the TTS audio cache at agent startup (alongside PERSISTENT_OPENERS), so the very first use of any variant plays as a ~300ms cache hit rather than a 2-3s Kokoro synth.

guardrails._doc: Cache fires ONLY if all conditions pass. Otherwise fall through to the LLM.
guardrails._skip_if_recent_tool_call_doc: Once any tool has been called in this conversation, the dialog is already inside a caller-specific or date-specific flow. Bail out of the cache to avoid disconnected answers.
guardrails._skip_after_turn_doc: After ~12 turns the conversation context is usually too deep for a canned answer to feel cohesive. Early-call factual Q&A is the sweet spot.
guardrails._skip_if_user_text_contains_doc: These words signal the caller is heading into a date/caller-specific flow (reservation lookup, availability check, booking). Always defer to the LLM.

pet_fee._doc: Caller asks specifically about pet/dog cost. Distinct from pet_dogs_allowed (yes/no policy question).
trigger_phrases: how much is the pet fee
how much does a dog cost
how much does a pet cost
how much extra for a dog
how much extra for a pet
how much do you charge for a dog
how much do you charge for pets
do you charge for a dog
do you charge for a pet
do you charge extra for pets
do you charge extra for a dog
is there a fee for pets
is there a fee for dogs
is there a fee for a dog
is there a charge for pets
is there a charge for dogs
is there an extra charge for pets
is there an extra charge for dogs
what's the charge for a dog
what is the charge for a dog
what's the cost for a dog
what is the cost for a dog
what do you charge for a dog
what do you charge for pets
pet_fee._stt_mishearings_doc: Common STT mishearings of 'pet fee' observed in real transcripts. Add new ones here when they appear.

responses.text: The pet fee is twenty dollars per stay for dogs.
Twenty dollars per stay for dogs.
There's a twenty-dollar pet fee per stay.
Twenty dollars per stay — and that covers up to two dogs for one week.
It's twenty dollars per stay. Cats aren't allowed, by the way.
Twenty dollars per stay for dogs. We don't accept cats.

pet_dogs_allowed._doc: Caller asks if dogs/pets are allowed at all. Yes/no policy question.
trigger_phrases: is it ok if i bring my dog
is it okay if i bring my dog
is it ok to bring a dog
is it okay to bring a dog
mind if i bring my dog
mind if i bring a dog

responses.text: Yes, dogs are welcome with a twenty-dollar fee per stay.
Yes, we welcome dogs. There's a twenty-dollar pet fee per stay.
Yes, you can bring a dog. The pet fee is twenty dollars per stay.
Dogs are welcome here — twenty-dollar fee per stay.
Yes, dogs are welcome. Twenty dollars per stay, and that covers up to two dogs.
Dogs are welcome — twenty-dollar fee per stay. Cats aren't allowed, though.
Yes, we're dog-friendly. Twenty dollars per stay.

trigger_phrases: is it ok if i bring my cat
is it ok to bring a cat
mind if i bring my cat

responses.text: I'm sorry, but we don't accept cats.
Unfortunately, cats aren't allowed.
Sorry, cats aren't allowed. We do welcome dogs, though.
No cats, unfortunately — but dogs are welcome.

pet_unattended._doc: Caller asks if a dog can be left in the room alone.
trigger_phrases: leave my dog in the room
leave the dog in the room
leave my dog in the crate
leave my dog in a crate
leave my dog in the kennel
leave my dog in a kennel
can my dog stay in the room
can i leave him in the room
can i leave her in the room

responses.text: No, dogs can't be left unattended in the room — even in a kennel or crate. We ask that they stay with you.
Please keep your dog with you — they can't be left alone in the room.
Dogs need to stay with their owner — we ask that they not be left alone in the room.

pet_damage_liability._doc: Caller asks about responsibility for pet-caused damage.
trigger_phrases: if my dog makes a mess

responses.text: Yes — the credit card on file may be charged for any pet-related damage, odor, stains, or extra cleaning. We just ask that you keep an eye on things.
We do charge the card on file for any damage or extra cleaning caused by a pet.

checkin_time._doc: Caller asks what time check-in is.
trigger_phrases: what time can i check in
what time should i check in
how early can i check in
when do you start check in

responses.text: Check-in is when the rooms are ready, typically around two PM until eight PM.
You can check in once your room is ready — usually around two PM. We're at the desk till eight.
Around two PM until eight. Sometimes rooms are ready earlier — feel free to call when you get to town.
Two until eight PM. Call ahead if you'll arrive after eight.
Two PM is typical, give or take depending on cleaning. Up till eight PM.

checkout_time._doc: Caller asks what time check-out is.
trigger_phrases: what time do i check out
what time do i need to check out
what time should i check out
what time must i check out
when do i have to leave
how late can i check out

responses.text: Check-out is at eleven AM.
Eleven in the morning.
Eleven AM is check-out.
Eleven AM — let us know in the morning if you need a bit more time.

wifi._doc: Caller asks about Wi-Fi or internet access.

responses.text: Free Wi-Fi is available everywhere in the building.
Yes — we have free Wi-Fi throughout the inn.
Free Wi-Fi, available everywhere.
Yes, free Wi-Fi all through the building.
Yes, a homemade breakfast is included with your stay.
Homemade breakfast, included with the stay.
Yes — breakfast is homemade, and it's included.
We do — a homemade breakfast comes with every stay.

coffee._doc: Caller asks about coffee availability or in-room coffee.

responses.text: Instant coffee is in the lobby twenty-four seven, and brewed coffee is in the dining room in the morning and through the day.
Instant in the lobby anytime, plus brewed in the dining room mornings and afternoons.
Coffee in the lobby twenty-four seven, brewed coffee in the dining room during the day.
Not in the rooms, but instant is in the lobby anytime and brewed is in the dining room mornings.
Yes — free self-parking, on site.
Free self-parking, right at the inn.
Self-parking is free and on site.

trailer_parking._doc: Caller asks about trailer or RV parking specifically.

responses.text: Yes — trailer-length parking is available for rooms twenty-five and twenty-seven. There's also trailer parking in the front lot and on the street in back.
Trailer parking is available — rooms twenty-five and twenty-seven have trailer-length spots, plus more space in the front lot and on the street behind the inn.
Smoking isn't allowed in the rooms — that includes cannabis. There's a three-hundred-dollar fee if smoking is detected.
No smoking inside the rooms, please. A three-hundred-dollar fee applies if it's detected.
We're a non-smoking inn — including cannabis. A three-hundred-dollar fee applies if there's evidence of smoking inside.

pool_spa_gym._doc: Caller asks about pool, hot tub, spa, gym, fitness facilities.

responses.text: We don't have a pool, hot tub, or gym on site.
No pool or hot tub, sorry — we're a smaller historic inn.
No pool, spa, or gym here. The inn focuses on the historic rooms and the included breakfast.

ev_charging._doc: Caller asks about EV charging on site.

responses.text: No on-site EV charging — but there are public chargers nearby in Florence, including a Tesla Destination Charger.
We don't have EV charging here, but there are public chargers in town a short drive away.
Yes, we have plenty of ice for your coolers and drinks.
Yep, ice is available — plenty for coolers or drinks.

in_room_amenities._doc: Caller asks about microwave, fridge, kitchen, stove. Static facts: rooms have microwaves and mini-fridges, no stoves.

responses.text: All our rooms have microwaves and mini-fridges. No stoves, though.
Every room has a microwave and a mini-fridge — no stove.
Microwaves and mini-fridges in every room. We don't have stoves.
The rooms don't have safes.
No in-room safes, unfortunately.

ai_identity._doc: Caller checks whether they're talking to a real person. KB design note: keep these emphatic and unsoftened — callers want a clear AI acknowledgment to reduce the uncanny-valley effect.
trigger_phrases: am i talking to a robot
am i talking to a computer
am i talking to a machine
am i talking to a real person
am i talking to a person
am i talking to a human

responses.text: Yes. I'm an artificial intelligence trained to answer the phones and make reservations.
Yes — I'm an A I, trained to help with questions and reservations.
Yes, I'm software — an A I trained to take calls and book rooms.

speak_to_human._doc: Caller explicitly asks for a human / person / the front desk. Cache speaks the verbal handoff ("Let me transfer you to the front desk now.") and the post_action field then triggers the actual transfer via IrisAgent._execute_transfer_with_fallback. On connected: Iris mutes and steps out. On no_answer: TRANSFER_FALLBACK_PHRASE is spoken so the caller has an explicit next step (message or Eric's cell). Triggers deliberately exclude bare-noun phrases like 'the front desk' / 'let me speak to' that produce too many false positives in non-transfer contexts (e.g. 'tell the front desk', 'let me speak to my wife').
trigger_phrases: get a person on the line
transfer me to the front desk

responses.text: Of course. Let me transfer you to the front desk now.
Sure, let me transfer you to the front desk now.
Of course — connecting you to the front desk.

multi_day_discount._doc: Caller asks about discounts for longer stays.

responses.text: Yes — we offer discounts for two-day, three-day, four-day, five-day, and weekly stays.
Yes, longer stays get a discount — two-night, three-night, on up through weekly rates.

senior_aarp_discount._doc: Caller asks about senior or AARP discount.

responses.text: Yes — we offer a five-percent discount to anyone who books over the phone, and that includes AARP and senior callers automatically.
Booking over the phone gets you a five-percent discount automatically — that covers AARP and senior rates.
Yes — we offer discounted weekly rates for stays of seven nights or longer.
Yes, weekly rates are available for stays of seven nights or more.

fresh_linens._doc: Caller asks about fresh linens / towels mid-stay.

responses.text: Yes, fresh linens are available during your stay — just request them in the morning.
Sure — request fresh linens in the morning and housekeeping will swap them out.

credit_card_required._doc: Caller asks if a credit card is required to book.
trigger_phrases: do you need a credit card
need a credit card to reserve

responses.text: Yes — we need a credit card to guarantee the reservation. A two-hundred-fifty-dollar hold may be placed as a security deposit.
Yes, a credit card is required to hold the room. There's a two-hundred-fifty-dollar security hold.

cash_payment._doc: Caller asks if cash is accepted.

responses.text: Yes, you can pay with cash at check-in. We still need a credit card on file, and either a two-hundred-dollar hold on it or a two-hundred-dollar cash deposit refunded at checkout.

near_river._doc: Caller asks how close the inn is to the river or Old Town.

responses.text: The inn is one block from the river and right in Old Town Florence.
One block from the river, in the Old Town area.

who_else_can_i_talk_to._doc: Caller asks if there are other people/agents they can talk to. Persona-specific responses: each persona names the OTHER personas + a real human. The pick filters by current persona automatically.
trigger_phrases: who else can i talk to
who else can i speak to
anyone else i can talk to
anyone else i can speak to

responses.text: I'm Iris, but you can also talk to Henry, or I can transfer you to a real person at the front desk.
I'm Iris — there's also Henry, or I can transfer you to the front desk.
I'm Henry, but you can also talk to Iris, or I can transfer you to a real person at the front desk.
I'm Henry — there's also Iris, or I can transfer you to the front desk.
I'm Aoede, but you can also talk to Iris or Henry, or I can transfer you to a real person at the front desk.
I'm Eric, but you can also talk to Iris or Henry, or I can transfer you to a real person at the front desk.

how_to_book._doc: Caller asks how to make a reservation. Cache fires only as a soft handoff — the actual booking flow goes to the LLM on the next turn.
trigger_phrases: how do i make a reservation
how can i make a reservation

responses.text: You can book directly with me on the phone right now — that gets you the best rate. We're also on our website and on Booking dot com.
I can book it for you right now over the phone — that's the best rate. You can also book online at our website or on Booking dot com.
